name: domain-crawl.sh refuses a path-prefix crawl whose seed sits at the root path so a whole-domain crawl can never happen by accident
command:
OUT=/tmp/cl564-r38-$$; mkdir -p $OUT && printf '

Root

seed content

' > $OUT/seed.html && SCOPE=$(printf '{"strategy": "path-prefix"}' | base64 | tr -d '\n'); bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/commands/domain-crawl.sh" --seed-url file:///index.html --seed-file $OUT/seed.html --sid r38-src --shape html-sections --scope-b64 "$SCOPE" --pages-dir $OUT/pages --manifest $OUT/crawl.jsonl 2>e.err; rc=$?; [ $rc -eq 2 ] && grep -q 'REFUSED sid=r38-src strategy=path-prefix with root path prefix' e.err && echo root-path-crawl-refused 1>&2; rm -rf $OUT
